zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Hastings於大約1296年出生在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾阿加文尼時，她的父親John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings35歲，母親Isabel de Valence33歲。她於1313年與Thomas d'Oyley Lord of Ranton結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1323年享年28歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Hastings was born about 1296, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings, was 35 and her mother, Isabel de Valence, was 33. She married Thomas d'Oyley Lord of Ranton in 1313. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1323, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Hastings est née environ 1296, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Valence, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas d'Oyley Lord of Ranton en 1313. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1323, à l’âge de 28 ans.

Lei sposato Thomas d'Oyley Lord of Ranton nel 1313. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1323, all'età di 28 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Hastings(이)가 대략 1296년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Valence(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1313년 Thomas d'Oyley Lord of Ranton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1323년 28세의 나이로 사망했다.
